annotations.label: Simplex

anchor.snippet: is a generalization of the notion of a triangle or tetrahedron to arbitrary dimensions

annotations.note: Mathlib defines a simplex as a bundled collection of `n+1` affinely independent points in affine space, the standard formalization of an n-simplex.

annotations.label: 0-simplex is a point

anchor.snippet: a 0-dimensional simplex is a point

annotations.note: `mkOfPoint` constructs a 0-simplex from a single point, and `range_mkOfPoint_points` shows its point set is a singleton.

annotations.label: 1-simplex is a line segment

anchor.snippet: a 1-dimensional simplex is a line segment

annotations.note: `stdSimplex_fin_two` proves the standard 1-simplex equals the segment between the two unit vectors.

annotations.label: 2-simplex is a triangle

anchor.snippet: a 2-dimensional simplex is a triangle

annotations.note: `Affine.Triangle` is defined as the abbreviation `Simplex k P 2`, a 2-simplex.

annotations.label: 3-simplex is a tetrahedron

anchor.snippet: a 3-dimensional simplex is a tetrahedron

annotations.note: The 3-simplex `Simplex k P 3` exists but Mathlib has no dedicated 'tetrahedron' object identifying it as such.

annotations.label: 4-simplex is a 5-cell

anchor.snippet: a 4-dimensional simplex is a 5-cell

annotations.note: The 4-simplex `Simplex k P 4` exists but there is no '5-cell' object in Mathlib.

annotations.label: k-simplex

anchor.snippet: is a k -dimensional polytope that is the convex hull of its k + 1 vertices

annotations.note: `Affine.Simplex k P n` is the k+1 affinely independent vertices, whose convex hull is the k-simplex.

anchors.snippet: is a k -dimensional polytope that is the convex hull of its k + 1 vertices

annotations.label: Regular simplex

anchor.snippet: is a simplex that is also a regular polytope

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of regular polytope or regular simplex.

annotations.label: Standard / probability simplex

anchor.snippet: is the k -dimensional simplex whose vertices are the k + 1 standard unit vectors

annotations.note: `stdSimplex` is the set of nonnegative vectors summing to 1, with `convexHull_rangle_single_eq_stdSimplex` showing it is the hull of the unit vectors.

anchors.snippet: is the k -dimensional simplex whose vertices are the k + 1 standard unit vectors

annotations.label: Simplicial complex

anchor.snippet: simplices to form a simplicial complex

annotations.note: `Geometry.SimplicialComplex` formalizes geometric simplicial complexes in a module over an ordered ring.

annotations.label: Abstract simplicial complex

anchor.snippet: a simplex is simply a finite set and the complex is a family of such sets that is closed under taking subsets

annotations.note: `AbstractSimplicialComplex` is a downward-closed family of nonempty finite sets containing all singletons.

annotations.label: Face of a simplex

anchor.snippet: is called a face of the simplex

annotations.note: `Affine.Simplex.face` is the subsimplex on a given subset of the vertices.

annotations.label: m-face, vertices, edges, facets

anchor.snippet: are called the vertices

annotations.note: Vertices are `points`, facets are `faceOpposite`, and m-faces are `face`, but the bundled terminology (edges, etc.) is not a single declaration.

annotations.label: Number of m-faces

anchor.snippet: the number of m -faces is equal to the binomial coefficient

annotations.note: No lemma counting the m-faces of a simplex by binomial coefficient was found.

annotations.label: Coface

anchor.snippet: is a coface of a simplex B if B is a face of A

annotations.note: No notion of coface exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Extended f-vector

anchor.snippet: The extended f-vector for an n -simplex can be computed by

annotations.note: No f-vector of a simplex/polytope is formalized.

annotations.label: Edge count is triangle number

anchor.snippet: The number of 1-faces (edges) of the n -simplex is the n -th triangle number

annotations.note: No edge-counting lemma for simplices was found.

annotations.label: Fewest vertices requiring n dimensions

anchor.snippet: is the polytope with the fewest vertices that requires n dimensions

annotations.note: This minimality property of the simplex is not formalized.

annotations.label: Join construction

anchor.snippet: can be constructed as a join

annotations.note: `convexJoin` gives the join of convex sets, but the specific construction of a simplex as a join of vertices is not stated.

annotations.label: (−1)-simplex

anchor.snippet: the empty set is defined to be a (−1)-simplex

annotations.note: `Affine.Simplex` requires `n : ℕ` (at least one point), so the empty (−1)-simplex is not represented.

annotations.label: Standard n-simplex

anchor.snippet: is the subset of R n +1 given by

annotations.note: `stdSimplex 𝕜 ι` is exactly `{f | (∀ x, 0 ≤ f x) ∧ ∑ x, f x = 1}`.

annotations.label: Simplex in affine hyperplane

anchor.snippet: lies in the affine hyperplane obtained by removing the restriction

annotations.note: `stdSimplex.sum_eq_one`/`stdSimplex_eq_inter` encode the `∑ = 1` hyperplane condition, but it is not phrased as an affine hyperplane embedding.

annotations.label: Vertices of standard n-simplex

anchor.snippet: The n + 1 vertices of the standard n -simplex are the points

annotations.note: `single_mem_stdSimplex` and `stdSimplex.vertex` give the vertices `Pi.single i 1`.

annotations.label: Standard simplex as 0/1-polytope

anchor.snippet: is an example of a 0/1-polytope

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of 0/1-polytope.

annotations.label: Canonical map to affine simplex

anchor.snippet: There is a canonical map from the standard n -simplex to an arbitrary n -simplex

annotations.note: `Set.Finite.convexHull_eq_image` realizes the convex hull as the image of `stdSimplex` under the weight-to-point map, but no packaged canonical map to an `Affine.Simplex` exists.

annotations.label: Barycentric coordinates

anchor.snippet: are called the barycentric coordinates of a point in the n -simplex

annotations.note: `AffineBasis.coord` is the family of barycentric coordinate affine maps.

annotations.label: Affine n-simplex

anchor.snippet: is often called an affine n -simplex

annotations.note: `Affine.Simplex` is precisely the affine n-simplex in affine space.

annotations.label: Generalized barycentric coordinates

anchor.snippet: These are known as generalized barycentric coordinates

annotations.note: Generalized barycentric coordinates for arbitrary convex polytopes are not formalized.

annotations.label: Softmax function

anchor.snippet: is the softmax function , or normalized exponential function

annotations.note: No softmax/normalized exponential function is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Δ0

anchor.snippet: is the point 1 in R 1

annotations.note: `stdSimplex_unique` shows the standard simplex on a one-element index is the single point `fun _ ↦ 1`.

annotations.label: Δ1

anchor.snippet: is the line segment joining (1, 0) and (0, 1) in R 2

annotations.note: `stdSimplex_fin_two` equals the segment between `Pi.single 0 1` and `Pi.single 1 1`.

annotations.label: Δ2

anchor.snippet: is the equilateral triangle with vertices

annotations.note: The standard 2-simplex `stdSimplex ℝ (Fin 3)` exists but its equilateral-triangle geometry is not proven.

annotations.label: Δ3

anchor.snippet: is the regular tetrahedron with vertices

annotations.note: The standard 3-simplex exists as `stdSimplex ℝ (Fin 4)` but its regular-tetrahedron geometry is not proven.

annotations.label: Δ4

anchor.snippet: is the regular 5-cell with vertices

annotations.note: The standard 4-simplex exists as `stdSimplex ℝ (Fin 5)` but its regular-5-cell geometry is not proven.

annotations.label: Increasing coordinates (indefinite sum)

anchor.snippet: An alternative coordinate system is given by taking the indefinite sum

annotations.note: This alternative increasing-coordinate parametrization is not formalized.

annotations.label: Ordered simplex fundamental domain, volume 1/n!

anchor.snippet: the ordered simplex is a (closed) fundamental domain for the action of the symmetric group on the n -cube

annotations.note: No result identifying the ordered simplex as a fundamental domain of `Sₙ` on the cube with volume `1/n!` was found.

annotations.label: Order-only presentation

anchor.snippet: it uses the order but not addition

annotations.note: This observation about the order-only coordinate presentation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Projection onto standard simplex

anchor.snippet: possibly with coordinates that are negative or in excess of 1

annotations.note: The Euclidean projection algorithm onto the standard simplex is not formalized.

annotations.label: Simplex as corner of cube

anchor.snippet: This yields an n -simplex as a corner of the n -cube

annotations.note: The corner-of-cube construction of a simplex is not formalized.

annotations.label: Inductive construction of regular simplex

anchor.section: Cartesian coordinates for a regular n -dimensional simplex in R n
anchor.snippet: One way to write down a regular n -simplex in R n is to choose two points

annotations.note: The inductive Cartesian construction of a regular simplex is not formalized.

annotations.label: Two choices for additional vertex

anchor.section: Cartesian coordinates for a regular n -dimensional simplex in R n
anchor.snippet: there are two choices for the additional vertex

annotations.note: This step of the regular-simplex construction is not formalized.

annotations.label: Centered unit-side-length vertices

anchor.section: Cartesian coordinates for a regular n -dimensional simplex in R n
anchor.snippet: It can be translated to the origin by subtracting the mean of its vertices

annotations.note: Centering a regular simplex at its vertex centroid is not formalized.

annotations.label: Inscribed hypersphere radius

anchor.section: Cartesian coordinates for a regular n -dimensional simplex in R n
anchor.snippet: This simplex is inscribed in a hypersphere of radius

annotations.note: Every simplex has a circumsphere (`circumsphere`/`mem_circumsphere`), but the specific radius formula for the regular simplex is not derived.

annotations.label: Inscribed in unit hypersphere

anchor.section: Cartesian coordinates for a regular n -dimensional simplex in R n
anchor.snippet: A different rescaling produces a simplex that is inscribed in a unit hypersphere

annotations.note: `circumradius` exists generally, but the unit-circumsphere rescaling of a regular simplex is not formalized.

annotations.label: Side length of unit-hypersphere simplex

anchor.section: Cartesian coordinates for a regular n -dimensional simplex in R n
anchor.snippet: The side length of this simplex is

annotations.note: The side-length formula for the unit-circumsphere regular simplex is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cyclic group construction

anchor.section: Cartesian coordinates for a regular n -dimensional simplex in R n
anchor.snippet: A highly symmetric way to construct a regular n -simplex is to use a representation of the cyclic group

annotations.note: The cyclic-group representation construction of a regular simplex is not formalized.

annotations.label: Sufficient condition for orbit to be regular simplex

anchor.section: Cartesian coordinates for a regular n -dimensional simplex in R n
anchor.snippet: A sufficient condition for the orbit of a point to be a regular simplex

annotations.note: This orbit condition for a regular simplex is not formalized.

annotations.label: Volume via determinant

anchor.snippet: The volume of an n -simplex in n -dimensional space with vertices

annotations.note: The determinant/n! volume formula for a simplex is not formalized (only the parallelepiped version exists).

annotations.label: Volume via Gram determinant

anchor.snippet: employs a Gram determinant and works even when

annotations.note: The Gram-determinant volume formula for simplices is not formalized.

annotations.label: Symmetric volume formula

anchor.snippet: A more symmetric way to compute the volume of an n -simplex

annotations.note: The symmetric homogeneous-coordinate volume formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Volume via Cayley–Menger determinant

anchor.snippet: is via the Cayley–Menger determinant

annotations.note: The Cayley–Menger determinant volume formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Parallelotope volume formula

anchor.snippet: it is the formula for the volume of an n - parallelotope

annotations.note: `Basis.addHaar_parallelepiped` gives the parallelepiped volume as `ENNReal.ofReal |b.det v|`.

annotations.label: Cube decomposition into n-path simplices

anchor.snippet: If P is the unit n -hypercube, then the union of the n -simplexes formed by the convex hull of each n -path is P

annotations.note: The decomposition of the hypercube into n! path simplices is not formalized.

annotations.label: General parallelotope decomposition

anchor.snippet: If P is a general parallelotope, the same assertions hold

annotations.note: The analogous parallelotope simplex decomposition is not formalized.

annotations.label: Volume under standard n-simplex

anchor.snippet: the volume under a standard n -simplex

annotations.note: The 1/n! volume of the region under the standard simplex is not formalized.

annotations.label: Volume of regular unit-side simplex

anchor.snippet: The volume of a regular n -simplex with unit side length is

annotations.note: The closed-form volume of a unit-side regular simplex is not formalized.

annotations.label: Dihedral angle of regular simplex

anchor.section: Dihedral angles of the regular n -simplex
anchor.snippet: they have the same dihedral angle of cos −1 (1/ n )

annotations.note: The arccos(1/n) dihedral angle of the regular simplex is not formalized.

annotations.label: n-dimensional Pythagorean theorem

anchor.snippet: there exists an n -dimensional version of the Pythagorean theorem

annotations.note: The de Gua / n-dimensional Pythagorean theorem for orthogonal-corner simplices is not formalized.

annotations.label: Pythagoras and de Gua's theorem cases

anchor.snippet: For a 2-simplex, the theorem is the Pythagorean theorem for triangles with a right angle

annotations.note: The 2-simplex (Pythagorean) case is formalized via the right-angle norm identity, but the 3-simplex de Gua case is not.

annotations.label: Hasse diagram isomorphic to hypercube edges

anchor.snippet: The Hasse diagram of the face lattice of an n -simplex is isomorphic to the graph of the ( n + 1) - hypercube 's edges

annotations.note: The face-lattice Hasse diagram / hypercube edge graph isomorphism is not formalized.

annotations.label: Simplex as vertex figure of hypercube

anchor.snippet: The n -simplex is also the vertex figure of the ( n + 1) -hypercube

annotations.note: Vertex figures of polytopes are not formalized.

annotations.label: Simplex equivalent to n-ball

anchor.snippet: an n -simplex is equivalent to an n -ball

annotations.note: Only the n=1 case (homeomorphism with the unit interval) is formalized; no general simplex-to-ball homeomorphism exists.

annotations.label: Simplex is manifold with corners

anchor.snippet: Every n -simplex is an n -dimensional manifold with corners

annotations.note: The simplex is not equipped with a manifold-with-corners structure in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Simplex as space of probability distributions

anchor.snippet: the points of the standard n -simplex in ( n + 1) -space form the space of possible probability distributions on a finite set

annotations.note: `stdSimplex` is exactly the nonnegative, sum-one vectors, but no explicit identification with `PMF`/probability distributions is provided.

annotations.label: Affine homeomorphism correspondence

anchor.snippet: This correspondence is an affine homeomorphism

annotations.note: No affine homeomorphism between the standard simplex and a space of probability distributions is formalized.

annotations.label: Aitchison geometry

anchor.snippet: is a natural way to construct an inner product space from the standard simplex

annotations.note: Aitchison geometry is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Self-dual compounds

anchor.snippet: Since all simplices are self-dual, they can form a series of compounds

annotations.note: Self-duality of simplices and polytope compounds are not formalized.

annotations.label: Two triangles form a hexagram

anchor.snippet: Two triangles form a hexagram

annotations.note: The hexagram compound is not formalized.

annotations.label: Stella octangula

anchor.snippet: Two tetrahedra form a compound of two tetrahedra or stella octangula

annotations.note: The stella octangula compound is not formalized.

annotations.label: Compound of two 5-cells

anchor.snippet: Two 5-cells form a compound of two 5-cells in four dimensions

annotations.note: The compound of two 5-cells is not formalized.

annotations.label: Simplicial complexes

anchor.snippet: simplices are used as building blocks to construct an interesting class of topological spaces called simplicial complexes

annotations.note: Both `AbstractSimplicialComplex` and the geometric `Geometry.SimplicialComplex` are formalized.

annotations.label: Simplicial homology

anchor.snippet: to define a certain kind of homology called simplicial homology

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes homology of simplicial sets/objects (and singular homology), but not classical simplicial homology of a simplicial complex directly.

annotations.label: Affine k-chain

anchor.snippet: is called an affine k - chain

annotations.note: The notion of a formal sum of affine simplices (affine chain) is not formalized as such.

annotations.label: Boundary of n-simplex is a chain

anchor.snippet: the boundary of an n -simplex is an affine ( n − 1) -chain

annotations.note: The alternating-face-map boundary operator is formalized for simplicial objects, but not specifically as an affine-chain boundary.

annotations.label: Boundary of boundary is zero

anchor.snippet: the boundary of the boundary of a simplex is zero

annotations.note: The alternating face map complex is a genuine chain complex (so `d ∘ d = 0`), but this is for simplicial objects, not the affine-simplex boundary.

annotations.label: Embedding into a manifold

anchor.snippet: a simplex (and a chain) can be embedded into a manifold

annotations.note: Embedding of simplices/chains into a manifold is not formalized.

annotations.label: Singular n-simplex

anchor.snippet: is frequently referred to as a singular n -simplex

annotations.note: `TopCat.toSSetObjEquiv` identifies n-simplices of the singular simplicial set with continuous maps from the standard topological simplex.

annotations.label: Algebraic standard n-simplex

anchor.snippet: the algebraic standard n-simplex is commonly defined as the subset of affine ( n + 1) -dimensional space

annotations.note: The algebro-geometric standard simplex (as a scheme/subvariety with `∑ tᵢ = 1`) is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: the algebraic standard n-simplex is commonly defined as the subset of affine ( n + 1) -dimensional space

annotations.label: Simplicial object assembly

anchor.snippet: the n -simplices for different dimensions n assemble into one simplicial object

annotations.note: `SimplicialObject`/`CosimplicialObject` is the general framework into which simplices of all dimensions assemble (e.g. `SimplexCategory.toTop`).
